ccel.title: ANF01. The Apostolic Fathers with Justin Martyr and Irenaeus

source.title: ANF01. The Apostolic Fathers with Justin Martyr and Irenaeus

title: XXXII.

source.title: ANF01. The Apostolic Fathers with Justin Martyr and Irenaeus

verses.book: Fragments from the Lost Writings of Irenæus
verses.text: Josephus says, that when Moses had been brought up in the royal palaces, he was chosen as general against the Ethiopians; and having proved victorious, obtained in marriage the daughter of that king, since indeed, out of her affection for him, she delivered the city up to him.

verses.book: Fragments from the Lost Writings of Irenæus
verses.text: Why was it, that when these two (Aaron and Miriam) had both acted with despite towards him (Moses), the latter alone was adjudged punishment?

verses.book: Fragments from the Lost Writings of Irenæus
verses.text: First, because the woman was the more culpable, since both nature and the law place the woman in a subordinate condition to the man.

verses.book: Fragments from the Lost Writings of Irenæus
verses.text: Or perhaps it was that Aaron was to a certain degree excusable, in consideration of his being the elder [brother], and adorned with the dignity of high priest.

verses.book: Fragments from the Lost Writings of Irenæus
verses.text: Then again, inasmuch as the leper was accounted by the law unclean, while at the same time the origin and foundation of the priesthood lay in Aaron, [the Lord] did not award a similar punishment to him, lest this stigma should attach itself to the entire [sacerdotal] race; but by means of his sister’s [example] He awoke his fears, and taught him the same lesson.

verses.book: Fragments from the Lost Writings of Irenæus
verses.text: For Miriam’s punishment affected him to such an extent, that no sooner did she experience it, than he entreated [Moses], who had been injured, that he would by his intercession do away with the affliction.

verses.book: Fragments from the Lost Writings of Irenæus
verses.text: And he did not neglect to do so, but at once poured forth his supplication.

verses.book: Fragments from the Lost Writings of Irenæus
verses.text: Upon this the Lord, who loves mankind, made him understand how He had not chastened her as a judge, but as a father; for He said, “If her father had spit in her face, should she not be ashamed?

verses.book: Fragments from the Lost Writings of Irenæus
verses.text: Let her be shut out from the camp seven days, and after that let her come in again.”
